title: Roland taps Sony CSL tech for ‘Melody Flip’ AI-music tool - Music Ally
text:
Roland is a giant of the instruments and music-software sector, but it also wants to be a player in the emerging AI-assisted creativity world. Witness its announcement overnight of
its new ‘Melody Flip’ tool
.
Developed with Sony Computer Science Laboratories – one of the pioneers of the modern AI-music sphere – it is described as “melody-generation software” designed to be used by musicians in their creative workflow.
How? By “letting creators import an audio file and automatically analyze its musical DNA, including the structure, BPM, key, chord progression, genre, mood, and more,” according to Roland’s announcement.
“It then pairs the analysis with a curated library of about 300 creative palettes (predefined musical styles and tonal directions) to generate fresh melodic ideas” – which producers can then play with.
The tool will be released for MacOS and WIndows as a plug-in for DAWs, initially as a free trial in May.
You can imagine some questions about the ethics of a tool that lets people upload audio files to use as the basis for new ideas. Does that include audio files of copyrighted music, for example?
That said, Roland has been very keen to position itself on the do-the-right-thing side of the AI music debates. In 2024, it teamed up with Universal Music Group to
publish some ‘Principles for Music Creation with AI’
, promising that “human-created works must be respected and protected”.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
